Answer directly with confident, spoiler-safe recommendations
requirements.gap: The prompt covers spoiler safety and tone, but not the direct-answer-first behavior and confident recommendation posture.

requirements.label: Show horizontal mentioned-shows strip from chat
Open Detail from mentions or Search fallback
Show six random starter prompts with refresh
Summarize older turns while preserving voice
Seed Ask-about-show sessions with show handoff
Emit `commentary` plus exact `showList` contract
Retry malformed mention output once, then fallback
Redirect Ask back into TV/movie domain
Treat concepts as taste ingredients, not genres

requirements.gap: The concepts flow is present, but the plan never explicitly frames concepts as taste ingredients rather than genre labels.

requirements.label: Return bullet-only, 1-3 word, non-generic concepts
Order concepts by strongest aha and varied axes
requirements.gap: The plan specifies shared, evocative concepts, but not strongest-first ordering or cross-axis variety.

requirements.label: Require concept selection and guide ingredient picking
requirements.gap: Concept selection is required, but the ingredient-picking guidance and nudges are not specified.

requirements.label: Return exactly five Explore Similar recommendations
Support full Alchemy loop with chaining
Clear downstream results when inputs change
requirements.gap: It clears downstream results when shows change, but not when concept selections themselves change.

requirements.label: Generate shared multi-show concepts with larger option pool
requirements.gap: Multi-show concepts are shared, but the plan does not explicitly provide a larger option pool than single-show generation.

requirements.label: Cite selected concepts in concise recommendation reasons
Deliver surprising but defensible taste-aligned recommendations
requirements.gap: Reasons cite concepts, but the surprise-without-betrayal quality bar is not captured as an acceptance criterion.

requirements.label: Keep one consistent AI persona across surfaces
requirements.evidence: `9.4 AI System Prompt Requirements`; `14.1 API Route`
requirements.gap: Ask and Scoop get compatible tones, but the plan does not explicitly define one shared persona across every AI surface.

requirements.label: Enforce shared AI guardrails across all surfaces
requirements.evidence: `9.4 AI System Prompt Requirements`; `14.1 API Route`
requirements.gap: AI constraints appear in Ask and Scoop sections, but the plan does not define one shared guardrail contract enforced across all AI routes.

requirements.label: Make AI warm, joyful, and light in critique
requirements.evidence: `9.4 AI System Prompt Requirements`; `14.1 API Route`
requirements.gap: Warmth and honesty are implied, but the joy-forward, light-in-critique voice pillars are not explicitly carried across surfaces.

requirements.label: Structure Scoop as personal taste mini-review
Keep Ask brisk and dialogue-like by default
requirements.evidence: `9.1 Chat UI`; `9.4 AI System Prompt Requirements`
requirements.gap: Ask is conversational, but the brisk dialogue-first default response style is not specified.

requirements.label: Feed AI the right surface-specific context inputs
requirements.gap: Ask and Scoop inputs are spelled out, but the plan does not fully define surface-specific context inputs for every AI surface.

requirements.label: Validate discovery with rubric and hard-fail integrity
requirements.gap: Real-show resolution is covered, but the broader quality rubric and hard-fail validation loop are not planned.

requirements.label: Show person gallery, name, and bio
Include ratings, genres, and projects-by-year analytics
Group filmography by year
Open Show Detail from selected credit
Include font size and Search-on-launch settings
Support username, model, and API-key settings safely
Export saved shows and My Data as zip
Encode export dates using ISO-8601

top_gaps.label: Re-add preserves My Data and refreshes public data: If removal truly deletes the record, users can lose prior tags, ratings, status, and Scoop history when they later save the same show again, which breaks a core durability promise

Enforce shared AI guardrails across all surfaces: Without one shared guardrail contract, Ask, Scoop, Alchemy, and Explore Similar can drift on spoiler safety, domain bounds, honesty, and actionable-output behavior
Make client cache safe to discard: If cache disposal safety is not planned explicitly, clearing local client state may appear to corrupt user data or make benchmark resets unreliable

Feed AI the right surface-specific context inputs: If each AI surface is not fed the right context, discovery quality will feel inconsistent and taste alignment will vary unpredictably across Ask, Scoop, Explore Similar, and Alchemy

Order concepts by strongest aha and varied axes: Without ordering and variety rules, concept chips can become repetitive or low-signal, weakening both Explore Similar and Alchemy recommendation quality

narrative.overall_posture: This is a strong implementation plan with notable semantic gaps rather than a weak or skeletal one. It is ready enough to guide substantial engineering work, but not yet precise enough to guarantee a benchmark-faithful rebuild without additional planning around durability and AI behavior contracts.
narrative.strength_clusters: The plan is strongest in Benchmark Runtime & Isolation, Collection Data & Persistence, Collection Home & Search, and the main scaffolding for Show Detail and Ask Chat. Those areas are broken into concrete phases, routes, repository functions, and component-level tasks rather than being left as broad feature headings.
narrative.weakness_clusters: The gaps cluster around two patterns. First, AI-specific behavioral contracts remain looser than the product spec in AI Voice, Persona & Quality and Concepts, Explore Similar & Alchemy. Second, a smaller number of lifecycle semantics are underspecified or internally inconsistent, especially around cache disposability, re-adding removed shows, and persistent shell/navigation expectations.
narrative.risk_assessment: If this plan were executed as-is, the app would probably demo well at first pass: the routes, schema, collection flows, and major surfaces are all there. The first serious QA failures would appear in edge-behavior trust loops: a remove-then-re-add flow that cannot clearly preserve prior My Data, and AI surfaces that feel inconsistent because their shared guardrails and context contracts are not fully pinned down.
narrative.remediation_guidance: The remaining work is mostly specification-tightening, not architectural rethinking. The plan needs a short follow-up pass that locks the exact data lifecycle for deletion, re-add, and disposable client state; then a shared AI contract layer that defines persona, guardrails, context inputs, and quality validation across all AI routes; then a final acceptance-criteria pass for navigation persistence, launch behavior, detail-page hierarchy, and concept ranking rules.
